---
title: "svm_analysis"
output: html_document
date: "2025-08-29"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyverse)
library(cowplot)
library(ggforce)
library(ggridges)
options(scipen = 999)
library(colorspace)
library(tidytext)    # for reorder_within() and scale_x_reordered()
```

#=========================================================================================
# VALIDATION RESULTS
#=========================================================================================


Loading validation results
```{r}
base_dir <- file.path("/Volumes/T7 Shield/svm_chpc_output/svm_model/")

# 1) Locate all per‐fold validation CSVs
files <- list.files(
  path       = base_dir,
  pattern    = "svm_rbf_results_excluded_fold_\\d+\\.csv$",
  recursive  = TRUE,
  full.names = TRUE
)

# 2) Read & combine
all_val_results <- files %>%
  set_names() %>% 
  map_dfr(~ {
    df <- read_csv(.x, show_col_types = FALSE)
    fold_num <- str_extract(basename(.x), "\\d+") %>% as.integer()
    df %>% mutate(fold = fold_num)
  })

```

plotting the validation results:
```{r}
ggplot(all_val_results, aes(x = num_features, y = validation, color = factor(fold))) +
  geom_line(size = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(
    name   = "Test fold",
    values = qualitative_hcl(
      n       = length(unique(all_results$fold)),
      palette = "Set 2"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Number of Features",
    y = "Average Validation Accuracy (%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

```



#=========================================================================================
# TEST RESULTS
#=========================================================================================

```{r}
library(tidyverse)

# 1) Point to the directory where your fold sub-folders live:
base_dir <- file.path("/Volumes/T7 Shield/svm_chpc_output/svm_model/")

# 2) Find all the CSV files (one per fold):
files <- list.files(
  path       = base_dir, 
  pattern    = "^svm_rbf_test_results_test_fold_\\d+\\.csv$", 
  recursive  = TRUE,
  full.names = TRUE
)

# 3) Read & combine, extracting the fold number from each filename:
all_results <- files %>% 
  set_names() %>% 
  map_dfr(read_csv, .id = "file", show_col_types = FALSE) %>% 
  mutate(
    fold = str_extract(basename(file), "\\d+")
  ) %>% 
  select(fold, num_features, test_accuracy)
```

Plotting the graph:
```{r}
ggplot(all_results, aes(x = num_features, y = test_accuracy, color = factor(fold))) +
  geom_line(size = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(
    name   = "Test fold",
    values = qualitative_hcl(
      n       = length(unique(all_results$fold)),
      palette = "Set 2"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Number of Features",
    y = "Test Accuracy (%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "right"
  )


```
Getting df with the average test accuracy:
```{r}
avg_results <- all_results %>%
  # if test_accuracy is character, first turn it numeric:
  mutate(test_accuracy = as.numeric(test_accuracy)) %>%
  group_by(num_features) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_test_accuracy = mean(test_accuracy, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

plotting the average test result:
```{r}
ggplot(
  data = avg_results,
  aes(x = num_features, y = avg_test_accuracy)
  ) +
  geom_line(size = 0.8, color = "darkmagenta") +
  labs(
    x = "Number of Features",
    y = "Average Test Accuracy (%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# ---- Method A: Elbow/knee on slope ----
slopes <- diff(avg_results$avg_test_accuracy) / diff(avg_results$num_features)
threshold <- 0.001  # e.g., 0.1% gain per 10 features (adjust as you wish)

idx <- which(slopes < threshold)
opt_n_elbow <- if (length(idx)) {
  # take the first time the marginal gain drops below threshold
  avg_results$num_features[idx[1] + 0]
} else {
  # fallback: never dropped → pick the largest tested (or choose your own rule)
  max(avg_results$num_features)
}

opt_n_elbow
```
